---
# ============================================================
#  HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE
#  1. Save a COPY of this file in the Activities/news/ folder.
#     Name it something short and descriptive, e.g. 2026-coring-svalbard.qmd
#  2. Fill in all the fields below (between the --- lines).
#  3. Replace the placeholder text in the body with your content.
#  4. Delete any sections you don't need.
#  5. Ask Ondrej to render and publish the site when you're done.
# ============================================================

title: "Activity Title"
# ↑ The title shown on the page and in the listing card.

date: 2030/01/01
# ↑ Date of the activity / news item. Format: YYYY/MM/DD

date-format: long
date-modified: last-modified

description: "A brief summary shown in the news listing card (aim for under 200 characters)."
# ↑ One or two sentences. This is what people see in the news grid BEFORE clicking through.

image: "../Materials/photos/placeholder.jpg"
# ↑ Path to a photo shown as the card thumbnail in the listing.
#   Place your photo in the Materials/photos/ folder and update the filename here.
#   Example:  image: "../Materials/photos/coring/svalbard-coring-2026.jpg"
#   You can leave the placeholder if you don't have a suitable image yet.

categories: [uit]
# ↑ Tags used for filtering. Use as many as you like, separated by commas.
#   Free-form examples:  [coring, fieldwork, svalbard]
#   Team tags that also appear on team pages: uit | oxford | eawag | salzburg | cuni
#   You can mix both:  [coring, fieldwork, uit, oxford]

sidebar: false
---

<!-- ============================================================
     BODY — replace all placeholder text below with your content.
     Lines starting with <!-- are comments and won't appear on the website.
     ============================================================ -->

Write a short introductory paragraph (2–4 sentences) giving readers the key
facts: what happened (or is happening), where, and why it matters.

<!-- KEY INFORMATION BOX — fill in the details below -->
::: {.callout-note}
## Key Information

📅 **Date:** DD Month YYYY

🗺️ **Location:** City, Country

🔗 **More info:** [Title of link](https://example.com)
<!-- To add more links, copy the line above and change the text and URL. -->
:::

## Background

Describe the context, motivation, and significance of this activity.
Why is it important for the MEMELAND project?

## Details

Describe what happened or what is planned. For events this might include
the programme, speakers, key findings, or outcomes.

<!-- HOW TO INSERT A PHOTO
     1. Place your image file in the Materials/photos/ folder (or subfolder).
     2. Use the syntax below. The text in quotes after the path is the
        "alt text" — a short description of the image for accessibility
        and for people whose images don't load. Always provide alt text.

     Single image, centred, with a caption:

     ![A scientist lowering a sediment corer through ice on a frozen lake in Svalbard.](../Materials/photos/your-photo.jpg){fig-align="center" fig-alt="A scientist lowering a sediment corer through ice on a frozen lake in Svalbard."}

     To add a visible caption underneath the image, add it after the closing }:
     : *Caption text here.*

     Two images side by side:

     ::: {layout-ncol=2}
     ![Left image alt text.](../Materials/photos/photo1.jpg)

     ![Right image alt text.](../Materials/photos/photo2.jpg)
     :::
-->

![Replace this with a description of your photo.](../Materials/photos/placeholder.jpg){fig-align="center" fig-alt="Replace this with a description of your photo."}

: *Caption: describe what is shown in the photo and credit the photographer if relevant.*

<!-- OPTIONAL: HOW TO INVOLVE OTHERS — delete this block if not needed -->
::: {.callout-tip}
## How to get involved

Describe any registration process, contact details, or next steps for
people who want to participate or learn more.
:::

## Resources

<!-- List any relevant links, documents, or publications below.
     Format:  - [Link text](https://full-url-here.com) -->

- [Relevant document or link](https://example.com)
